title: potentilla inclinata var. adscendens
intro: Potentilla inclinata var. adscendens, also known as dwarf potentilla or creeping potentilla, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant with yellow flowers, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating wounds, ulcers, and skin conditions due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Potentilla inclinata var. adscendens include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, tinctures, or salves, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
